content_markdown:
Venus and Earth may be regarded as behaving as black bodies. The mean temperature at the surface of Venus is about $600\text{ K}$ and at the surface of Earth is about $300\text{ K}$. Which of the following is the best estimate for the ratio

$$\frac{\text{power radiated per unit area on Earth}}{\text{power radiated per unit area on Venus}}?$$

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present, only text and mathematical expressions.

vibe_explanation: According to the Stefan-Boltzmann law, the power radiated per unit area (intensity) of a black body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature ($P/A = \sigma T^4$). Given $T_{\text{Earth}} = 300\text{ K}$ and $T_{\text{Venus}} = 600\text{ K}$, the ratio of temperatures is $1/2$. Therefore, the ratio of power radiated per unit area is $(1/2)^4 = 1/16$.
